findings.message: The new `review_status === 'sent'` short-circuit breaks the reusable whole-hostel lane. `savePrimeReviewDraft` is the mechanism that resets that singleton thread back to `pending` for legitimate future broadcasts (`apps/prime/functions/lib/prime-review-drafts.ts:126-150`); returning `{ outcome: 'already_sent' }` before that call would cause every post-first-send compose attempt to be treated as a duplicate, contradicting the document's later claim that the next compose can create a fresh draft.

The proposed idempotency guard is still not atomic, so it does not actually meet the stated goal that two identical `prime-compose` calls must not produce two broadcast messages. Two overlapping requests can both read a non-`sent` status, both run `savePrimeReviewDraft`, and both enter `sendPrimeReviewThread`; there is no lock or compare-and-swap around the pre-check, and the whole-hostel draft save rewrites the thread to `pending` (`apps/prime/functions/lib/prime-review-drafts.ts:126-150`, `apps/prime/functions/lib/prime-review-send.ts:63-76`).

The response contract is still internally inconsistent. The chosen path switches duplicate retries to HTTP 200 `{ outcome: 'already_sent' }`, but this row, the end-state table at line 147, and TASK-04 at line 159 still describe a 409-based flow. Planning and test work will diverge unless the document is normalized to one contract.

The Reception blast radius is understated. `sendPrimeInboxThread` is still used by the live Prime-send route at `apps/reception/src/app/api/mcp/inbox/[threadId]/send/route.ts:69`, and broadcast-specific coverage already exists in `apps/reception/src/app/api/mcp/__tests__/inbox-actions.route.test.ts:499-535`; the validation section only calls out `prime-compose` and `initiatePrimeOutboundThread` tests.
